title: Bless’d are the pure in heart
firstLine: Bless’d are the pure in heart,
publicDomainBasis: John Keble (1792–1866), Charles Lockhart (1745–1815). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Bless’d are the pure in heart,
For they shall see our God;
The secret of the Lord is theirs,
Their heart is Christ’s abode.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
The Lord, who left the heavens,
Our life and peace to bring,
To dwell in lowliness with men,
Their pattern and their King.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
He to the lowly soul
Doth still Himself impart,
And for His dwelling and His throne
Chooseth the pure in heart.

sections.label: Verse 4
